requestedDecision: For each of the sixteen signals TI-01..TI-16 in the docs/test-integrity.md classification table: is the assigned label (deterministic fact / tool-derived measurement / advisory interpretation), confidence-when-fired, false-positive risk, and enforcement level (rejected / advisory only / shadow candidate #22 / conditional shadow / gate implemented) correct and honest, or should any cell change? Specifically challenge: (a) any advisory signal that could be over-promoted to deterministic fact or vice versa, (b) the shadow-candidate set {TI-05, TI-06, TI-07, TI-08, TI-09, conditional TI-11} versus the advisory-only set {TI-10, TI-13, TI-14, TI-15}, (c) the outright rejections {TI-12 mutation score, TI-16 authoritative LLM verdict}, and (d) whether any falsifiable must-fire/must-not-fire test in the document contradicts its own signal's classification. Output: per-signal verdict (agree / change with proposed new value and reason), separated into facts, options, recommendation, uncertainty, and decisions that must remain human.
requiredActions: TI-12 mutation-score decline: change enforcement level from rejected to conditional shadow, restricted to repositories that already run a pinned mutation tool as a configured check with comparable scores; no default mutation run.

TI-13 tolerance widening and TI-15 weaker-matcher substitution: change enforcement level from advisory only to shadow candidate (#22) at lower priority than TI-05/TI-06/TI-09, with confidence split into deterministic rule-match versus advisory weakening interpretation.
TI-11 coverage decline: split confidence wording into measurement accuracy under identical tool/version/config/scope versus interpretation that the decline indicates weakening.

TI-01 configured-check change: narrow false-positive-risk wording to detection of a definition change without inferring intent, and strengthen the must-not-fire case to an unrelated .rigor/policy.json edit.
TI-08 snapshot churn: add a must-not-fire case covering snapshot-only changes without accompanying implementation changes, since the trigger is conjunctive.
TI-02/TI-03/TI-04: state explicitly that the implemented gate constrains progress classification (reduced/incomparable), not verification or merge outcomes.
